{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks about the characteristic of orthodontic wire that describes energy storage capacity. Among the options, resilience refers to the ability of a material to absorb energy and return to its original shape after deformation, which aligns with the concept of energy storage. Formability relates to the ease of shaping the material, range typically refers to the extent of motion or variation, and proportional limit is related to the stress-strain relationship up to which a material behaves linearly. Therefore, resilience is the most appropriate characteristic for describing energy storage capacity.", "answer_choice": "B"}
